pattern: Validating a token needs only three running counters -- a digit check, a hyphen-position check, and a trailing-punctuation check -- evaluated in a single left-to-right scan.
hints: Split on single spaces first, but remember that consecutive spaces create empty tokens you must skip entirely.
For each token, reject immediately the moment you see any digit; otherwise track how many hyphens and how many punctuation marks you've seen so far.

A hyphen is only legal as a single interior character with a lowercase letter on each side, and a punctuation mark is only legal as the very last character with at most one total -- fail the token the instant either rule is broken.

edge_inputs.description: hyphen at the very start of a token
multiple consecutive spaces between two tokens
a token near the maximum sentence length made only of letters
two punctuation marks in one token
punctuation appears in the middle instead of at the end
a digit hidden inside an otherwise word-like token
two hyphens in a single token

stress.description: randomly generated tokens (words, hyphenated words, punctuation, and invalid digit tokens) joined into a sentence near the length limit
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    limit = 1000
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    pieces = []
    cur_len = 0
    first = True
    while True:
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.55:
            wlen = rng.randint(1, 8)
            tok = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(wlen))
            if rng.random() < 0.3 and len(tok) > 1:
                pos = rng.randint(1, len(tok) - 1)
                tok = tok[:pos] + '-' + tok[pos:]
            if rng.random() < 0.3:
                tok += rng.choice('!.,')
        elif r < 0.8:
            wlen = rng.randint(1, 6)
            tok = ''.join(rng.choice(letters + '0123456789') for _ in range(wlen))
        else:
            tok = rng.choice('!.,')
        sep = '' if first else (' ' * rng.randint(1, 2))
        add = sep + tok
        if cur_len + len(add) > limit - 1:
            break
        pieces.append(add)
        cur_len += len(add)
        first = False
        if len(pieces) >= 100:
            break
    s = ''.join(pieces) if pieces else 'a'
    return [s]
